https://doc.rust-lang.org/rustdoc/write-documentation/documentation-tests.html#using--in-doc-tests

When I tried doing 

/// ```rust
/// func_that_might_fail()?;
/// # Ok(())
/// ```

The testing function failed with a very cryptic error message, any chance that we can make this work?
what error do you receiove? the ? can be used inside the function that return a Option or Result, so the code will compile with rustc so I assume that the error is caused by the following problem?
I posted the error in the #Help channel see the above link
I think the python script cannot handle a trailing expression (just my guess)
Yeah, I have to change the script a bit (Andreas also reported it, I think), will do.

Miguel Ojeda said:

Yeah, I have to change the script a bit (Andreas also reported it, I think), will do.

for what the script is used in this case?

Miguel Ojeda said:

Yeah, I have to change the script a bit (Andreas also reported it, I think), will do.

Earlier discussion here https://rust-for-linux.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/288089-General/topic/doctest
By the way, this was done in https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/912.

for what the script is used in this case?

It captures the code via rustdoc and transforms them into KUnit tests, i.e. it allows us to run the code inside the kernel with the rest of the C ones, plus it allows to use any kernel-only dependencies.
